questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In CCP, there are 8 tetrahedral sites and all are present completely inside the unit cell. There are 13 octahedral sites out of which 1 is at the body centre and 12 are at edge centres. Like CCP, another packing is HCP, in which the number of tetrahedral sites that are completely inside one unit cell is x . Mark the value of $x^2$.
[Assume those sites are completely inside the cell that are not shared between unit cells]
questions.solution:
Hint : H.C.P. contain 20 T.H site
Solution :
In H.C.P., 8 T.V are inside the unit cell while 12 are at vertical edges (2 on each vertical edge)
$\text { T.V. }=8+12 \times \frac{1}{3}=12$

One edge in H.C.P. contributes $\frac{1}{3}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The fourth co-ordination number of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ion is CsCl is x whereas fourth co-ordination number of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ion in NaCl is $y$. What is the value of $x+y$ ?
questions.solution:
CsCl has BCC like structure. The $4^{\text {th }}$ co-ordination number of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ion is 24
$\therefore x=24$

NaCl has FCC structure . The $4^{\text {th }}$ co-ordination number of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ion is 6
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore y=6 \\
& x+y=24+6=30
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\mathrm{BaTiO}_3$ has perovskite structure. In this structure, $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ occupies the corners while $\mathrm{Ti}^{+4}$ occupies the centre of the lattice. If the oxide co-ordination number of $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ ion is x , and that of $\mathrm{Ti}^{4+}$ is y , the $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ ion co-ordination number of oxide ion is $z$ and $\mathrm{Ti}^{4+}$ ion co-ordination number of oxide ion is $t$, then what is the value of ( $x+y+$ $z+t) ?$
questions.solution:
In $\mathrm{BaTiO}_3$ crystal
x, co-ordination no. of $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ ion $=12$
$y$, co-ordination no. of $\mathrm{Ti}^{4+}$ ion $=6$
z , co-ordination no. of $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$ ion w.r.t. $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}=4$
t , co-ordination no. of $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$ ion w.r.t. $\mathrm{Ti}^{4+}=2$
$\therefore x+y+z+t=12+6+4+2=24$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In crystal of NaCl , consider the particle $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$which is present at topmost layer. If x is the number of $2^{\text {nd }}$ nearest neighbours and $y$ is the number of $3^{\text {rd }}$ nearest neighbours with respect to reference particle. Calculate the value of $(x+y)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x=8\left(\text { at distance } \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \\ & y=4\left(\text { at distance } \frac{\sqrt{3} a}{2}\right) \\ & x+y=12\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
$A$ compound $A B$ has a rock salt structure with $A: B=1: 1$. The formula weight of $A B$ is $6.023 Y$ amu. And the closest distance between $A$ and $B$ is $Y^{1 / 3} \mathrm{~nm}$. The observed density of the lattice in $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ 
is :
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Iron crystallizes in several modifications. At about $910^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, the body centred cubic $\alpha$-form undergoes a transition to the face centred cubic $\gamma$-form. Assuming that the distance between nearest neighbours is same in the two forms at the transition temperature. Calculate the ratio of density of $\gamma$-iron to that of $\alpha$ iron at the transition temperature.
questions.solution:
In bcc structure ( $\alpha$ form) of iron
$4 r=\sqrt{3} a$
$\mathrm{n}=2$ atoms per unti cell from (one atom from 8 corners, one atom from the body centre)
So, $\rho(\gamma$-form $)=\frac{n \times M_m}{a^3 \times N_0}=\frac{2 \times M_m}{\left(\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}} r\right)^3 \times N_0}$     ....(i)
In fcc structue ( r form) of iron
$4 r=\sqrt{2} a$
$\therefore a=2 \sqrt{2} r$
$\mathrm{n}=4$ atoms per unit cell
$\therefore \rho(\gamma$ form $)=\frac{4 \times M_m}{(2 \sqrt{2} r)^3 \times N_0}...(ii)$
Dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i)
$\frac{\rho(y \text { form })}{\rho(\alpha \text { form })}=\frac{2}{\left(\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}} r\right)^3} \times \frac{(2 \sqrt{2} r)^3}{4}=1.0887$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Titanium crystallizes in a face centred cubic lattice. It reacts with C or H interstitially, by allowing atoms of these elements to occupy holes in the host lattice. Hydrogen occupies tetrahedral holes, but carbon occupies octahedral holes.
(a) Predict the formula of titanium hydride and titanium carbide formed by saturating the titanium lattice with "foreign" element.
(b) What is the maximum ratio of "foreign" atom radius to host atom radius that can be tolerated in a tetrahedral hole without causing a strain in the host lattice?
questions.solution:
(a) There are 4 atoms per unit cell and 8 tetrahedral sites per unit cell.
So, the ratio of atoms to tetrahedral sites $=1: 2$
So, the formula would be $\mathbf{T i H}_{\mathbf{2}}$
For carbide,
Since carbon occupies octahedral holes
So, ratio of octahedral hole to atom $=1: 1$
The formula of carbide is TiC
(b) Since for tetrahedral hole, the limiting ratios, radius ratio
$\frac{r^{+}}{r^{-}}=0.225$ to 0.414
Without causing a strain in the host lattice, i.e. $\frac{r^{+}}{r^{-}}=0.225$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a face centred lattice with all the positions occupied by A atoms the body centred octahedral hole in it is occupied by an atom B of an appropriate size for such a crystal, calculate the void space per unit volume of unit cell. Also predict the formula of the compound.
questions.solution:
Let a be the edge of the cube so that $\begin{aligned} & 4 r_A=\sqrt{2} a \\ & a=2 \sqrt{2} a_A\end{aligned}$ Now since the atoms B has occupied the body centre octaheedral void
$\begin{aligned} & 2 r_A+2 r_B=a \\ & 2 r_A+2 r_B=2 \sqrt{2} r_A\end{aligned}$ $2 \mathrm{rB}=(2 \sqrt{2}-2) \mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{A}} ; \frac{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{B}}}{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{A}}}=\sqrt{2}-1=1.414-1=0.414$ Volume of the cube $a^3=16 \sqrt{2} r_A^3$ Volume occupied by A and $\mathrm{B}=$ $\left[4 \times \frac{4}{3} \pi r_A^3\right]+\left[\frac{4}{3} \pi r_B^3\right]=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(4 r_A^3+r_B^3\right)$ Volume occupied per unit volume of unit cell $\phi=\frac{4 / 3 \pi\left(4 r_A^3+r_B^3\right)}{16 \sqrt{2} r_A^3}=\frac{\pi}{12 \sqrt{2}}\left[4+\left(\frac{r_B}{r_A}\right)^3\right]$ $=\frac{3.14}{1.2 \times 1.414}\left(4+(0.414)^3\right)=\frac{3.14 \times 4.071}{12 \times 1.414}=0.75$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A compound $A B$ has zinc blende structure with ' $A$ ' atoms at corners and face centre of the unit cell. If all the atoms present in one body diagonal are removed, the formula of the compound becomes $A_x B_y$. The sum $(x+y)$ is:
questions.solution:
A present in corners and face centre. $B$ present in alternate tetrahedral voids.
No. of A per unit cell $=6 \times \frac{1}{8}+6 \times \frac{1}{2}=\frac{15}{4}$
No. of B per unit cell $=4-1=3$
$\begin{aligned}& x: y=\frac{15}{4}: 3=5: 4 \\& x+y=5+4=9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
